Narrative: CT SINUS / FACIAL WITHOUT CONTRAST EXAM DATE: 9/15/2025 2:34 PM. COMPARISON: None
INDICATION: Punch in the face two weeks ago and has had difficulty breathing through her nose due to the
swelling. TECHNIQUE: 1.25 mm Axial CT images of the face were obtained in bone and soft tissue algorithm.
Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also examined. DOSE REPORT: This study involved (1) CT
acquisition(s). The CTDIvol and DLP values are included below as required by state law: 1; Series: 4; Head16 cm;
CTDIvol: 25 mGy; DLP: 600 mGy-cm For further information on CT radiation dose, see
http://www.ucdmc.ucdavis.edu/radiology/RadiationDose.html FINDINGS: Facial fractures: Fracture of the right
nasal bone without significant
displacement. Orbits: No evidence of acute orbital fracture. No evidence of vitreous or post septal hemorrhage.
Paranasal Sinuses and mastoid air cells: Partial opacification of the anterior ethmoid air cells. Soft tissues: No
significant subcutaneous edema. Visualized brain parenchyma: No significant abnormality is noted. IMPRESSION:
1. Fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement, which may be acute or chronic in nature. 2.
Otherwise, no evidence of acute facial fracture.
I have personally reviewed the images of this study and agree
with the above report. Final Report Electronically Signed By: Matthew Bobinski, M.D. OFF

Subjective
Nicole is a 35 year old female here today for Referral (To ent, ) and Appointment (Med refill acyclovir,
losartan & nicotine patches)
The patient is here today for a follow-up visit to request several medications and some testing. Patient needs
medication refills. Also is due for updated screening labs. Requesting STD testing. Her recent male sexual
partner tested positive for syphilis. She did not know at the time of intercourse. Condoms were not used.
Currently she has recurrent genital herpes. She needs refills on her acyclovir. She is alert and oriented.
Appears fatigued.
Appears older than stated age. Patient also has a nasal fracture which occurred 2 months ago. She believes
that it did not assault. Her stepfather has come to her home and share her face. She was seen in the ER and
police report was filed. She missed her initial follow-up visit with PCP. She was able to get a visit today and is
diagnosis. requesting an urgent referral to ENT for her nasal fracture. Sinus CT scan completed 2 months ago confirms
Vital uncomfortable. signs show elevated BP today. Other vitals are within normal range. She appears fatigued and
Please note: Patient was advised to keep a organize list of concerns for each appointment. Also needs to
have an organized digital or paper file for her health issues.
Review of Systems
Constitutional:
See HPI.

claude_vision.referral_reason: Urgent referral to ENT (Otolaryngology) for evaluation and management of a closed fracture of the nasal bone with delayed healing, subsequent encounter (S02.2XXG). The fracture occurred approximately 2 months prior to the referral date (12/12/2025), caused by blunt trauma (assault by stepfather). Patient reports difficulty breathing through her nose due to swelling.
relevant_clinical_findings: 35-year-old female with nasal fracture due to blunt trauma (assault) approximately 2 months prior to referral
Patient reports difficulty breathing through her nose secondary to swelling
Nasal congestion and rhinorrhea noted on physical exam (12/12/2025)
CT Sinus/Facial without contrast (performed 09/15/2025) confirmed fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement
Patient was seen in the ER at time of injury and a police report was filed
Patient missed her initial follow-up visit with PCP after the injury
Diagnosis coded as S02.2XXG — fracture of nasal bones, subsequent encounter for fracture with delayed healing
claude_vision.imaging_summary: CT Sinus/Facial Without Contrast performed on 09/15/2025. Key findings: Fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement (may be acute or chronic in nature). No evidence of acute orbital fracture. No vitreous or post-septal hemorrhage. Partial opacification of anterior ethmoid air cells. No significant subcutaneous edema. Impression: (1) Fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement; (2) No other evidence of acute facial fracture. Report signed by Matthew Bobinski, M.D. CT report IS included in the faxed documents (pages 8 of 15).
missing_information: No ER visit records attached despite mention of an ER encounter at time of injury
No imaging from the ER visit is included
Referral scheduling status at time of triage shows 0 visits scheduled (1 visit completed per the referral summary, but the ENT consultation note/outcome is not included in this fax)
Authorization number field is blank on the authorization document (auth# not populated in the services table)
No ENT-specific follow-up notes or outcomes from the completed visit are included

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent
provider_urgency_label.source: Multiple locations: (1) Referral form from Elica Health Centers lists 'Priority: Urgent [2]'; (2) Insurance authorization document (page 3 of fax) lists 'Priority Status: 1 - URGENT'; (3) Referral scheduling notes in the Sutter Health system list 'Priority: Urgent'; (4) Scheduling comments in the referral notes show attachment labeled 'URG' on both 12/16/2025 and 12/18/2025 entries; (5) Fax cover sheet from Elica Health Centers is marked 'URGENT'

evidence: CT performed 09/15/2025 confirms nasal fracture. Referral signed 12/12/2025 — injury occurred approximately 2 months prior, well outside the 1-2 week acute surgical window for nasal fractures.

Diagnosis coded as S02.2XXG: 'fracture of nasal bones, subsequent encounter for fracture with delayed healing' — the 'delayed healing' and 'subsequent encounter' coding explicitly confirms this is NOT an acute presentation.
Referring provider note states: 'Patient has fracture of the nasal bone, with delayed healing. Urgent referral sent to ENT.' — provider acknowledges delayed healing yet still labeled urgent.
criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent — marked in multiple locations including referral form ('Priority: Urgent [2]'), insurance authorization ('Priority Status: 1 - URGENT'), fax cover sheet (URGENT checkbox marked), and scheduling note attachments labeled 'URG'.
criteria_check.reasoning: The referring provider marked this referral as urgent across multiple document fields; however, the nasal fracture occurred approximately 2 months before the referral date and is explicitly coded as a subsequent encounter with delayed healing (S02.2XXG), placing it well outside the 1-2 week acute window that qualifies nasal fractures as an ENT urgent criterion — therefore no defined urgent criteria are met, and secondary approval is needed to confirm whether the provider's urgency label is clinically justified.

claude_vision.next_steps: Secondary review needed — provider marked this referral urgent across multiple fields, but the nasal fracture is approximately 2 months old with delayed healing (S02.2XXG), which is past the 1-2 week acute window for urgent nasal fracture criteria; triage team should contact referring provider Dr. Nancy Ekeke (916-454-2345) to confirm clinical justification for urgent designation before scheduling.
claude_vision.summary: This is a referral from Elica Health Centers (Dr. Nancy Ekeke, MD) to Sacramento Ear, Nose & Throat for a 35-year-old female with a confirmed right nasal bone fracture with delayed healing that occurred approximately 2 months ago due to assault. The referring provider has labeled the referral urgent across multiple documents, and a CT scan confirming the fracture is included; however, because the injury is approximately 2 months old and coded as a delayed-healing subsequent encounter, it falls outside the defined 1-2 week acute window that qualifies nasal fractures as an ENT urgent criterion. Secondary approval is required to verify whether the urgent designation is clinically warranted before scheduling.
